diagrams.title: Message Call Structure and Flow
diagrams.description: Shows the components of a message call and how data flows from caller to callee
elements: Calldata: function selector (4 bytes) + ABI-encoded arguments
Return Data Flow back to sender
diagrams.purpose: Visualize the internal transaction structure and execution context of message calls in Ethereum

diagrams.title: Call vs Delegatecall vs Staticcall Comparison
diagrams.description: Three-pane comparison showing execution contexts for each call type
elements: STATICCALL: Storage=Recipient, NO state modifications allowed
diagrams.purpose: Understand when to use each call type and their implications on execution context

diagrams.title: Reentrancy Attack Diagram
diagrams.description: Shows how reentrancy exploit works and the CEI solution
elements: Victim Contract: balance mapping, withdraw() function
Attacker calls withdraw() → balance check passes
Attacker contract's receive() calls withdraw() again BEFORE balance updated
Solution: CEI - Update state first, THEN call external
diagrams.purpose: Visualize the reentrancy vulnerability and how to prevent it with proper coding patterns
